name: HMS Ellesmere (FY 204)
events: 15 Jan 1941 HrMs O 9 (Lt. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown together with HMS Shemara (Cdr.(Retd.) H. Buckle, RN) and HMS Ellesmere (Lt. H. Roach, RNR). (1)

20 Dec 1941 At 1640 A/20, HMS P 38 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, DSC, RN) departed Plymouth for her 3rd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off Cherbourg, France in case the German capital ships at Brest might make a run up the Channel towards Germany.
P 38 was to proceed from the end of Q.Z.S. 278 [swept channel] through position 180° - Start Point - 15 miles to patrol in the vicinity of position 49°53'N, 01°47'W.
She was escorted out by HMS Ellesmere (T/Lt. A.C. Munro, RNVR). (2)
2 Mar 1943 Around 1805 A/2, HMS Taku (Lt. A.J.W. Pitt, RN) ended her 21th war patrol at Plymouth.
She was escorted in by HMS Ellesmere (T/Lt. A.C. Munro, RNVR) which had joined her around 1110 A/2. (3)

24 Apr 1944 HMS Seraph (Lt. T. Russell-Walling, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Plymouth with HMS Grassholm (T/Lt. J.A. Antonsen, RNR), HMS Minalto (A/Skr.Lt. A.S.H. Robb, RD, RNR), HMS Asie (A/Ch.Skr. F. Oldman, RNR) and HMS Ellesmere (T/Lt. A.C. Munro, RNVR). (4)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
